steps.title: Identify the key facts
steps.content: The passage states that only about 6% of bodies imaged by HST are within the solar system — meaning roughly 94% of its observations are of objects outside the solar system. Young and colleagues recommend a new telescope "dedicated exclusively to solar-system observations."

steps.title: Derive the implication
steps.content: If a brand-new instrument is needed specifically for solar-system work, the implication is that HST is not and will not be doing that work as its main focus. Its primary use will remain what it already is: observing extrasolar objects.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'will likely continue to be used primarily to observe objects outside the solar system': matches the 6%/94% data and the need for a separate dedicated telescope. ✓
  • Choice B — 'will no longer be used to observe solar system objects': overreach — the recommendation is to add a new telescope, not to bar HST from any solar-system observation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'can be modified to observe the features': overreach — the passage never mentions modifying HST; the researchers want a new, separate instrument. ✗
  • Choice D — 'lacks the sensors to observe the wavelengths': contradicts the passage, which says HST has revolutionized visible and UV imaging. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
